name: 彼得·伊里奇·柴可夫斯基
brief: 柴可夫斯基出生于俄罗斯沃特金斯克，原本学习法律并在司法部工作，23岁才决定全身投入音乐。他与富孀梅克夫人保持了长达14年的通信与资助关系，两人约定永不见面。他的婚姻（与安东尼娜·米柳科娃）是一场灾难，仅维持了数周便导致他精神崩溃。柴可夫斯基一生与忧郁和孤独为伴，他的死因至今成谜——官方说法是霍乱，但也有自杀的说法。
briefEn: Tchaikovsky was born in Votkinsk, Russia, and originally studied law, working in the Ministry of Justice before devoting himself entirely to music at the age of twenty-three. He maintained a fourteen-year correspondence and patronage arrangement with the wealthy widow Nadezhda von Meck, on the condition that they would never meet. His marriage to Antonina Miliukova was a disaster, lasting only weeks before triggering a nervous breakdown. Tchaikovsky was haunted by melancholy and loneliness throughout his life; the cause of his death remains a mystery — the official account cites cholera, though some believe it was suicide.
contribution: 柴可夫斯基将俄罗斯音乐带上了世界舞台。他创造性地融合了俄罗斯民间音乐的忧郁气质与西欧交响乐的精密结构，形成了独特的俄罗斯交响风格。他几乎凭一己之力将芭蕾音乐从伴奏性质的配乐提升为可以独立欣赏的交响性艺术——《天鹅湖》《睡美人》《胡桃夹子》至今仍是全球上演频率最高的芭蕾舞剧。
contributionEn: Tchaikovsky brought Russian music onto the world stage. He creatively fused the melancholic spirit of Russian folk music with the structural precision of Western European symphonic writing, forging a distinctly Russian orchestral style. Almost single-handedly, he elevated ballet music from functional accompaniment to a symphonic art worthy of the concert hall — Swan Lake, The Sleeping Beauty, and The Nutcracker remain the most frequently performed ballets worldwide.
style: 柴可夫斯基的音乐以极度的情感外露著称——他的旋律直接诉诸人心，毫不掩饰痛苦、狂喜、渴望和绝望。他的管弦乐配器色彩丰富而辉煌，善于用弦乐的长线条旋律与管乐的明亮色彩营造强烈的戏剧效果。他的音乐结构有时被认为不如德奥传统严密，但这恰恰使他的音乐具有一种自由而动人的叙事力量。
styleEn: Tchaikovsky's music is distinguished by its unrestrained emotional intensity — his melodies speak directly to the heart, making no effort to conceal anguish, ecstasy, yearning, or despair. His orchestration is vivid and brilliant, adept at pairing sweeping string cantilenas with the bright colors of the winds to create powerful dramatic effect. His formal structures are sometimes considered less rigorous than those of the Austro-German tradition, but this very freedom gives his music a compelling narrative force.

famousScores.name: 《天鹅湖》 Op.20
famousScores.desc: 芭蕾音乐的最高成就，白天鹅的主题举世闻名
famousScores.descEn: The pinnacle of ballet music; the White Swan theme is recognized the world over
famousScores.detail:
《天鹅湖》于1876年受莫斯科帝国剧院委托创作，首演于1877年2月20日，然而这次首演是一场灾难性的失败。第一位指挥德里戈将乐谱大幅删改，编舞雷丁格尔的舞蹈设计也受到批评，整个演出反响冷淡。柴可夫斯基本人对这次首演深感失望，他甚至没有在日记中提及此事。《天鹅湖》真正的辉煌要等到他去世之后——1895年，马林斯基剧院以彼季帕的编舞重新制作，终于获得了震撼人心的成功。

作品中「奥德泰主题」（白天鹅主题）的创作过程有一段小故事：据说柴可夫斯基是在一次乡村漫步时偶然看到一群白鸟掠过湖面，那个瞬间给了他创作灵感。这个主题以双簧管吹出，在弦乐的映衬下宛如天鹅在月光下的湖面滑翔，是整个芭蕾音乐文献中最令人心碎的旋律之一。开幕式上俄罗斯领导人在这段音乐下亮相已成为一种政治传统——「天鹅湖时刻」甚至成为苏联和俄罗斯政治危机期间的国际媒体用语。

《天鹅湖》在音乐史上的意义在于它永久性地改变了芭蕾舞剧的地位：从此芭蕾不再是轻娱乐，而是可以与歌剧和交响曲并列的严肃艺术形式。这部芭蕾的影响还延伸到了流行文化——从迪士尼动画到现代舞台演出，白天鹅的形象已成为全人类共同的文化财产。
famousScores.detailEn:
Swan Lake was commissioned by the Imperial Theater in Moscow in 1876 and premiered on February 20, 1877 — to disaster. The conductor Riccardo Drigo drastically cut and rearranged the score, the choreography by Reisinger was poorly received, and the whole production fell flat. Tchaikovsky was deeply disappointed and did not even mention the premiere in his diary. The ballet's true triumph came only after his death: the 1895 revival at the Mariinsky with Petipa's choreography was electrifying and changed everything.

The creation of the 'Odette theme' (the White Swan melody) has a small anecdote attached: Tchaikovsky is said to have been inspired by the sudden sight of a group of white birds skimming across a lake on a country walk. The theme, played by the oboe over string accompaniment, evokes a swan gliding across moonlit water and is among the most heartbreaking melodies in all ballet music. The sequence of a Russian leader's appearance to this theme has become a political tradition — 'Swan Lake moments' has even entered international media vocabulary to describe Russian and Soviet political crises.

Swan Lake permanently changed ballet's standing in music history — from light entertainment to a serious art form equal to opera and symphony. Its influence extends deep into popular culture: from Disney animation to contemporary stage productions, the white swan has become a shared cultural property of all humanity.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Tchaikovsky Swan Lake Ernest Ansermet Suisse Romande

famousScores.name: 《第一钢琴协奏曲》降b小调 Op.23
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Concerto No. 1 in B-flat minor, Op. 23
famousScores.desc: 开篇的宏伟序奏是古典音乐中最壮丽的时刻之一
famousScores.descEn: Its majestic opening introduction is one of the most thrilling moments in all of classical music
famousScores.detail:
1874年圣诞节前夕，柴可夫斯基将这首协奏曲的草稿弹给他最信任的音乐朋友、莫斯科音乐学院院长尼古拉·鲁宾斯坦听。鲁宾斯坦的反应令柴可夫斯基终生难忘：他不仅一言不发地听完全曲，之后更以极其尖刻的语言将其批得体无完肤，称之为「粗俗、平庸、无可救药」，并拒绝演奏这部作品。柴可夫斯基愤而宣布一字不改，将协奏曲的献辞从鲁宾斯坦改为汉斯·冯·彪罗。

首演于1875年10月25日在波士顿，由彪罗担任独奏。演出大获成功，彪罗随即在美国多个城市巡演，将这部协奏曲推向了世界。当首演成功的消息传回莫斯科，鲁宾斯坦据说深感惭愧，主动找到柴可夫斯基道歉，并将这首协奏曲加入了自己的演奏曲目。多年后，鲁宾斯坦成为这部协奏曲的最积极推广者之一，命运的讽刺至此完成了一个完整的圆圈。

《第一钢琴协奏曲》的开篇序奏——那段辉煌的弦乐主题加上宏大的钢琴和弦——是整个古典音乐曲目中被引用和改编最多的片段之一，仅次于贝多芬《第五交响曲》的开头。它对拉赫玛尼诺夫《第二钢琴协奏曲》的宏壮开篇有直接影响，也影响了格里格、格拉纳多斯等作曲家的协奏曲写作策略。
famousScores.detailEn:
On Christmas Eve 1874, Tchaikovsky played the draft of this concerto for his most trusted musical friend, Nicholas Rubinstein, director of the Moscow Conservatory. Rubinstein's reaction was unforgettable: he listened in total silence, then unleashed a scorching critique, calling it 'vulgar, commonplace, and beyond repair,' refusing to perform it. Tchaikovsky declared he would not change a note and transferred the dedication from Rubinstein to Hans von Bülow.

The premiere took place on October 25, 1875, in Boston, with Bülow as soloist. It was a triumphant success; Bülow immediately toured it to multiple American cities. When news of the triumph reached Moscow, Rubinstein was reportedly deeply ashamed, sought out Tchaikovsky to apologize, and added the concerto to his own programs. Years later Rubinstein became one of its most active champions — the irony of fate completing a perfect circle.

The opening introduction of the First Concerto — that sweep of blazing string theme crowned by majestic piano chords — is one of the most quoted and arranged passages in all classical music, second only to the opening of Beethoven's Fifth Symphony. Its direct influence on the grand opening of Rachmaninoff's Second Piano Concerto is unmistakable, and it shaped the concerto-opening strategies of Grieg, Granados, and others.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Tchaikovsky Piano Concerto 1 Van Cliburn Kondrashin 1958

famousScores.name: 《胡桃夹子》 Op.71
famousScores.desc: 圣诞季的必备芭蕾，《花之圆舞曲》家喻户晓
famousScores.descEn: The quintessential Christmas ballet; the Waltz of the Flowers is known to audiences everywhere
famousScores.detail:
《胡桃夹子》完成于1892年，是柴可夫斯基的最后一部芭蕾作品，改编自霍夫曼的童话故事《胡桃夹子与鼠王》。首演于1892年12月18日圣彼得堡马林斯基剧院，获得了观众的热烈欢迎，但当时的评论界对芭蕾本身（尤其是第一幕的大段哑剧）颇有批评，倒是对柴可夫斯基的音乐给予了高度评价。今天的《胡桃夹子》形象在很大程度上是1954年乔治·巴兰钦在纽约城市芭蕾的里程碑制作所塑造的。

《胡桃夹子》中有一个令人津津乐道的乐器故事：柴可夫斯基在「糖梅仙子之舞」中使用了刚刚问世不久的钢片琴（Celesta），那种空灵甜美的音色令他大为着迷。他在1891年访问巴黎时意外发现了这件新乐器，随即秘密订购了一台，并在写给友人的信中叮嘱对方千万不要把消息泄露给其他俄国作曲家——他怕李姆斯基-科萨科夫抢先使用这种新音色。这段「乐器秘密」成为音乐史上的趣事。

《胡桃夹子》对西方圣诞文化的影响深入骨髓——在北美，圣诞节期间演出这部芭蕾已成为一百余年的传统，许多芭蕾舞团一年的票房收入有近一半来自《胡桃夹子》的圣诞演出。《花之圆舞曲》和《糖梅仙子之舞》是全球最广为人知的古典旋律之一，其文化渗透力甚至超过了许多交响名作。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Nutcracker was completed in 1892, Tchaikovsky's final ballet, adapted from Hoffmann's fairy tale The Nutcracker and the Mouse King. The premiere at the Mariinsky on December 18, 1892, was warmly received by audiences, though critics were mixed about the ballet itself (particularly the long mime sequences in Act I), while praising Tchaikovsky's score highly. The Nutcracker as we know it today was largely shaped by George Balanchine's landmark 1954 production at the New York City Ballet.

A wonderful instrument story surrounds the ballet: Tchaikovsky used the newly invented celesta for the Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy — its ethereal sweetness enchanted him. Discovering the instrument during a Paris visit in 1891, he secretly ordered one and wrote to a friend begging him not to tell any other Russian composers, fearing Rimsky-Korsakov would use its unique timbre before him. This 'instrument secret' has become a beloved anecdote in music history.

The Nutcracker's influence on Western Christmas culture is profound: in North America, staging it during the holiday season has been a tradition for over a hundred years, and many ballet companies earn close to half their annual box office from Nutcracker performances. The Waltz of the Flowers and the Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy are among the most universally recognized melodies in the world, their cultural penetration exceeding many celebrated symphonic works.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Tchaikovsky Nutcracker André Previn London Symphony

famousScores.name: 《第六交响曲「悲怆」》 Op.74
famousScores.desc: 首演后九天柴可夫斯基即去世，绝望中的最后告别
famousScores.descEn: Tchaikovsky died just nine days after its premiere — a final farewell steeped in despair
famousScores.detail:
《悲怆交响曲》首演于1893年10月28日圣彼得堡，由柴可夫斯基本人亲自指挥；九天后，11月6日，他突然去世，死因至今成谜。官方公告为霍乱，但许多历史学家认为他是被一个「法庭荣誉审判」所迫，被迫服毒自杀——据说因为他与一位贵族子弟的情感关系被举报，一个由贵族组成的「荣誉法庭」裁定他必须用死亡来挽回声誉。真相可能永远无法确知。

交响曲的标题「悲怆」是柴可夫斯基的弟弟莫德斯特在首演后不久建议的，柴可夫斯基欣然采用。整部交响曲以一个不寻常的安排收尾：传统交响曲通常以热烈的快板终章收场，但《悲怆》以一个缓慢凄凉、渐渐消失于沉默中的慢板（第四乐章）作为终结，令听众在一片死寂中离开音乐厅。这个安排被许多人视为柴可夫斯基对自己即将到来的死亡的预感或告白。

《悲怆交响曲》是整个浪漫主义时代「死亡交响曲」传统的顶点——它不仅仅是一部描绘死亡的作品，它本身的历史语境（首演九天后作者即去世）已将它与死亡的主题永久地融为一体。它影响了马勒、肖斯塔科维奇以及所有在20世纪尝试以交响曲直面人类苦难与死亡的作曲家。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Pathétique Symphony premiered in St. Petersburg on October 28, 1893, conducted by Tchaikovsky himself; nine days later, on November 6, he was dead. The official cause was cholera, but many historians believe he was forced to take his own life after an 'honor tribunal' of nobles — convened in response to a reported relationship with a noble youth — ruled that his death was necessary to preserve reputations. The truth may never be known.

The title 'Pathétique' was suggested by Tchaikovsky's brother Modest shortly after the premiere, and Tchaikovsky gladly adopted it. The symphony ends with a structure unlike any before it: rather than the conventional energetic finale, the Pathétique closes with a slow, desolate Adagio lamentoso (fourth movement) that fades into silence, leaving the audience to depart in stillness. Many have seen this as Tchaikovsky's premonition or confession of his imminent death.

The Pathétique Symphony stands at the apex of the Romantic 'death symphony' tradition — it is not merely a work depicting death, but one whose historical context (the composer's death nine days after the premiere) has permanently fused it with that theme. Its influence on Mahler, Shostakovich, and all who have used the symphony in the twentieth century to confront human suffering and mortality is immeasurable.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Tchaikovsky Symphony 6 Pathétique Yevgeny Mravinsky Leningrad

famousScores.name: 《D大调小提琴协奏曲》 Op.35
famousScores.nameEn: Violin Concerto in D major, Op. 35
famousScores.desc: 与贝多芬、勃拉姆斯并称三大小提琴协奏曲
famousScores.descEn: Ranked alongside Beethoven's and Brahms's as one of the three great violin concertos
famousScores.detail:
《小提琴协奏曲》创作于1878年，是柴可夫斯基在灾难性婚姻崩溃后在瑞士疗养期间所作，是他心情最自由的时期之一。这部作品是在他的年轻朋友、小提琴家约瑟夫·科泰克的帮助下完成的——科泰克每天与柴可夫斯基一同研究小提琴演奏技术，并建议了许多独奏声部的写法。协奏曲原来献给科泰克，后来改献给著名小提琴家利奥波德·奥尔。

然而奥尔在看到总谱后以「演奏上不可能实现」为由拒绝首演这部作品，导致协奏曲被搁置了三年。1881年12月4日，小提琴家阿道夫·布罗茨基在维也纳爱乐的演出中完成了世界首演。著名评论家爱德华·汉斯利克（贝多芬-勃拉姆斯传统的卫道士）在评论中写下了音乐史上最恶毒的评语之一：「音乐变成了臭音」——然而正是这条恶评让协奏曲迅速引发了广泛关注，起到了适得其反的宣传效果。

《小提琴协奏曲》的第一乐章宏大的旋律和热烈的情感是柴可夫斯基对自由与激情的最纯粹表达；第二乐章「如歌的行板」（Canzonetta）是整个协奏曲文献中最温柔的旋律之一。这部作品对弗拉基米尔·霍洛维茨、帕格尼尼等演奏家的职业生涯和艺术理念产生了深远影响，也是古典音乐进入通俗文化圈的重要桥梁。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Violin Concerto was composed in 1878 during Tchaikovsky's Swiss convalescence following the collapse of his disastrous marriage — one of his most liberated creative periods. It was written with the help of his young friend, the violinist Iosif Kotek, who worked through the technical demands of the solo part with Tchaikovsky daily and suggested much of the writing for the violin. The concerto was originally dedicated to Kotek, later changed to the famous violinist Leopold Auer.

Auer, on receiving the score, rejected it as 'unplayable,' leaving the concerto in limbo for three years. On December 4, 1881, the violinist Adolf Brodsky gave the world premiere with the Vienna Philharmonic. The eminent critic Eduard Hanslick, guardian of the Beethoven-Brahms tradition, wrote one of music history's most venomous reviews: 'the music stinks to the ear' — yet the notoriety of this review rapidly drew widespread attention to the concerto, backfiring spectacularly on its author.

The First movement's sweeping melodies and passionate emotion represent Tchaikovsky's purest expression of freedom and exhilaration; the slow movement Canzonetta is one of the most tender melodies in all concerto literature. This work deeply influenced the careers and artistic ideals of performers across generations and has served as an important bridge between classical music and popular audiences worldwide.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Tchaikovsky Violin Concerto David Oistrakh Eugene Ormandy

famousScores.name: 《1812年庄严序曲》 Op.49
famousScores.desc: 以大炮和教堂钟声庆祝俄国抵御拿破仑侵略，史上最「响亮」的管弦乐作品
famousScores.descEn: Celebrating Russia's repulsion of Napoleon with cannon fire and church bells — the loudest orchestral work in history
famousScores.detail:
《1812年庄严序曲》创作于1880年，原为莫斯科艺术工业博览会以及救世主大教堂（建于纪念拿破仑战争胜利）落成典礼而作。柴可夫斯基本人对这部作品评价甚低——他在给梅克夫人的信中写道：「这是一首没有任何深意、只是图一乐的响亮热闹的序曲，我写它并没有用多少心」。这种自我贬低恰恰成为后世的笑谈：这首他最不重视的作品成为了他最广为人知的作品之一。

作品结尾处要求使用真正的大炮和教堂钟声的记谱，这一要求在历史上引发了无数有趣的演出方式。1962年，伯恩斯坦在华盛顿国会大厦前的户外音乐会上使用了真实的大炮实弹演出，引发轰动。冷战期间，这部作品在西方作为「俄国精神」的象征被频繁使用，具有强烈的政治意涵；在苏联，这部描绘俄国民族荣光的作品同样是重要的文化道具。

《1812年序曲》是古典音乐进入流行文化最成功的案例之一。它在美国每年7月4日独立日的烟火晚会上几乎是标配，使一代又一代美国听众通过这首曲子认识了「古典音乐」。从音乐史角度来看，这部作品还代表了19世纪「标题管弦乐」对爱国主义叙事的全力运用，直接影响了后来的好莱坞战争音乐和20世纪的史诗配乐传统。
famousScores.detailEn:
The 1812 Overture was composed in 1880 for the Moscow Arts and Industry Exhibition and the consecration of the Cathedral of Christ the Savior, built to commemorate the Napoleonic Wars victory. Tchaikovsky himself thought little of it, writing to Mme von Meck: 'It will be very loud and noisy, but I wrote it without warmth or love, and therefore there is probably no artistic merit in it.' This self-deprecation became a historical irony: the work he cared least about became one of his most famous.

The score's instruction to use actual cannons and church bells has generated endless performance creativity. In 1962, Leonard Bernstein achieved a sensation at an outdoor concert near the U.S. Capitol with live artillery fire. During the Cold War, the work was frequently deployed in the West as a symbol of the 'Russian spirit' carrying strong political overtones; in the Soviet Union it served equally as a vehicle for national glory.

The 1812 Overture is one of the most successful cases of classical music crossing into popular culture. In America it is nearly mandatory at July 4th fireworks concerts, introducing classical music to generation after generation. Historically it represents the nineteenth-century tradition of 'program orchestral music' at its most patriotically explicit, directly influencing Hollywood war scoring and the twentieth-century epic soundtrack tradition.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Tchaikovsky 1812 Overture Georg Solti Chicago Symphony

famousScores.name: 《罗密欧与朱丽叶》幻想序曲
famousScores.desc: 莎士比亚悲剧的音乐化身，爱情主题是柴可夫斯基最美的旋律之一
famousScores.descEn: A musical embodiment of Shakespeare's tragedy; the love theme is one of Tchaikovsky's most beautiful melodies
famousScores.detail:
《罗密欧与朱丽叶》幻想序曲的创作经历了三次重要修订（1869、1870、1880年），最终定稿于1880年。这部作品是在巴拉基列夫（「强力集团」领袖）的建议和具体指导下创作的：巴拉基列夫不仅提出了以莎士比亚悲剧为素材的构想，还对早期版本提出了详细的修改建议，其影响程度可谓亦师亦友。这段创作过程体现了当时俄罗斯音乐界两个阵营（民族主义与欧洲化传统）之间的复杂互动。

作品中的「爱情主题」（弦乐奏出的那段著名旋律）是整个柴可夫斯基作品中最令人心旷神怡的旋律之一，被无数电影、电视和商业广告引用。有趣的是，巴拉基列夫在听完第一稿后对「爱情主题」的评价是「还不够好」，催促柴可夫斯基修改。柴可夫斯基照办，改出来的版本正是今天我们熟悉的那首旋律——有时候来自同行的压力确实能激发出作曲家的最高水平。

《罗密欧与朱丽叶》幻想序曲是柴可夫斯基交响诗写作中最成功的作品，也是他早期创作中技巧最成熟的一首。作品以普罗科菲耶夫、柏辽兹以前莎士比亚音乐化的最经典范本而著称，对于如何将戏剧冲突（蒙太古与卡普莱特的仇恨、罗密欧与朱丽叶的爱情、命运的悲剧结局）转化为奏鸣曲式的三主题对立与发展提供了教科书般的示范。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Romeo and Juliet Fantasy Overture went through three major revisions (1869, 1870, 1880), reaching its final form in 1880. It was composed at the suggestion and under detailed guidance of Balakirev, leader of the Mighty Handful: Balakirev not only proposed the Shakespearean subject but gave specific revision notes on early drafts, acting as teacher and collaborator. The creative process embodies the complex interplay between the two camps of Russian music at the time — nationalist and Europeanist.

The work's 'love theme' — that celebrated string melody — is among the most ravishing melodies in all Tchaikovsky and has been quoted by countless films, television programs, and advertisements. Interestingly, Balakirev's initial response to the first draft was that the love theme was 'not good enough,' pressing Tchaikovsky to revise it. Tchaikovsky complied, and the result was the melody we know today — proof that peer pressure can sometimes push a composer to their finest achievement.

Romeo and Juliet is Tchaikovsky's most successful symphonic poem and his most technically polished early work. It stands as the classic model — before Prokofiev or Berlioz — of musical Shakespeare, providing a textbook demonstration of how dramatic conflict (the Montague-Capulet feud, the lovers' passion, the tragic destiny) can be translated into the opposing themes and development of sonata form.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Tchaikovsky Romeo and Juliet Karajan Berlin Philharmonic

famousScores.name: 《第五交响曲》e小调 Op.64
famousScores.nameEn: Symphony No. 5 in E minor, Op. 64
famousScores.desc: 命运动机贯穿四个乐章，从阴暗宿命到凯旋胜利的壮丽旅程
famousScores.descEn: A fate motif threading through all four movements — a journey from dark destiny to triumphant affirmation
famousScores.detail:
《第五交响曲》完成于1888年，是柴可夫斯基交响曲中结构上最「德奥」式的一首——命运动机（开篇的降A大调低沉旋律）像贝多芬的「命运」动机一样贯穿四个乐章，在每次出现时都呈现出不同的情绪色彩。柴可夫斯基在笔记本中写道：「全面服从命运，即天意——对信仰不置可否……是否应该放弃信仰？」这些文字揭示了这部交响曲的哲学核心：人与命运之间的斗争与和解。

第二乐章的圆号独奏是整个圆号文献中最著名的独奏段落之一——那个深沉而充满忧郁的主题令无数听众第一次感受到圆号的歌唱能力。终乐章以E大调的凯旋性尾声（命运动机转化为壮阔的长调进行曲）收场，带来压倒一切的情感释放。然而许多音乐家批评这个结尾「太过做作」——柴可夫斯基本人在首演后的日记中也承认：「这部交响曲有某种令我不满意的地方，尾声太强人意志了。」

《第五交响曲》在全球演出频率统计中长期位居柴可夫斯基交响曲首位，是世界各地交响乐团曲目单上最频繁出现的俄国交响曲。它对肖斯塔科维奇的交响曲写作（尤其是以贯穿性动机组织整部交响曲的结构方法）有直接影响，也是许多入门听众接触古典交响曲的第一站。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Fifth Symphony was completed in 1888 and is the most structurally 'Austro-German' of Tchaikovsky's symphonies — a fate motif (the brooding opening theme in A-flat major) permeates all four movements like Beethoven's fate motif, appearing each time in a different emotional guise. Tchaikovsky wrote in his notebook: 'Complete surrender to fate, or, what is the same thing, to the inscrutable predestination of Providence... Should I cast myself in the embrace of faith?' These words reveal the symphony's philosophical core: the struggle and reconciliation between the individual and fate.

The second movement's horn solo is among the most celebrated solos in all horn literature — its deep, melancholy cantilena introduced generations of listeners to the singing capacity of the French horn. The finale ends with an E major triumph (the fate motif transformed into a sweeping major-key march), bringing overwhelming emotional release. Yet many musicians have criticized this ending as 'too willed.' Tchaikovsky himself wrote in his diary after the premiere: 'Something about this symphony does not satisfy me. The ending is too forced.'

The Fifth Symphony consistently tops performance-frequency statistics for Tchaikovsky symphonies worldwide and is the most frequently programmed Russian symphony in the orchestral repertoire. It directly influenced Shostakovich's symphonic writing, especially the structural method of organizing a whole symphony around a single pervasive motif, and remains a first port of call for many listeners newly discovering the symphonic form.

famousScores.name: 《睡美人》 Op.66
famousScores.desc: 柴可夫斯基自认为最好的芭蕾作品，结构最完整、音乐最精致
famousScores.descEn: Tchaikovsky's own favourite of his three ballets — his most structurally complete and musically refined
famousScores.detail:
《睡美人》完成于1889年，是柴可夫斯基与编舞大师玛丽斯·彼季帕合作最为完美的一次。与《天鹅湖》首演的灾难性遭遇不同，《睡美人》从一开始就获得了成功：1890年1月15日在马林斯基剧院的首演大受好评，沙皇亚历山大三世亲临观看，事后仅对柴可夫斯基说了一个字：「好。」这个极简的王室评语令柴可夫斯基颇为不满，但也成为了音乐史上的趣谈。

柴可夫斯基本人曾在多次场合表示，《睡美人》是他认为最好的一部芭蕾，理由是它的音乐结构最为严整，每一段音乐与舞蹈动作之间的配合最为精妙。「玫瑰慢板」（Rose Adagio，第一幕公主与四位王子共舞的场景）被芭蕾界公认为对女主角平衡技术要求最高的段落之一；序曲和婚礼场面的音乐则是柴可夫斯基管弦乐写作中最精致、最贵气的段落。

《睡美人》对20世纪芭蕾的影响通过巴兰钦和狄亚吉列夫传承至今。1921年狄亚吉列夫在伦敦上演了豪华版《睡美人》，尽管商业上的巨大失败几乎令他的俄罗斯芭蕾舞团破产，但这次制作在艺术上对英国芭蕾界的影响是决定性的。这部芭蕾的旋律渗透了迪士尼动画电影《睡美人》（1959年），通过音乐将柴可夫斯基带给了20世纪的全球儿童。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Sleeping Beauty was completed in 1889, the most perfectly realized collaboration between Tchaikovsky and the great choreographer Marius Petipa. Unlike the disastrous premiere of Swan Lake, The Sleeping Beauty was a success from the start: the premiere at the Mariinsky on January 15, 1890, was warmly received. Tsar Alexander III attended and offered Tchaikovsky a single word of response: 'Very nice.' This minimal royal comment privately disappointed Tchaikovsky but has become a cherished anecdote in music history.

Tchaikovsky himself stated on multiple occasions that The Sleeping Beauty was his finest ballet, praising its musical structure as the most coherent of his three and its coordination of music and choreography as the most exquisite. The Rose Adagio (Princess Aurora dancing with four princes in Act I) is regarded by the ballet world as one of the most technically demanding balance passages for a ballerina; the overture and wedding scene contain some of the most refined and sumptuous orchestral writing in Tchaikovsky's output.

The Sleeping Beauty's influence on twentieth-century ballet was transmitted through Balanchine and Diaghilev. Diaghilev's lavish 1921 London production, though a commercial disaster that nearly bankrupted the Ballets Russes, was artistically decisive for British ballet. And the ballet's melodies, transplanted wholesale into Disney's animated Sleeping Beauty (1959), brought Tchaikovsky's music to children around the world.

famousScores.name: 《D大调第一弦乐四重奏》 Op.11（如歌的行板）
famousScores.nameEn: String Quartet No. 1 in D major, Op. 11 (Andante cantabile)
famousScores.desc: 第二乐章「如歌的行板」是托尔斯泰落泪的旋律，最感人的俄罗斯室内乐
famousScores.descEn: The Andante cantabile second movement moved Tolstoy to tears — the most affecting piece of Russian chamber music
famousScores.detail:
《第一弦乐四重奏》完成于1871年，第二乐章「如歌的行板」（Andante cantabile）是整部四重奏中最广为人知的段落，也是整个弦乐四重奏文献中最令人动容的乐章之一。这个乐章的主题来源于一首乌克兰民歌，据说是柴可夫斯基在卡明卡庄园度假时偶然听到一位泥瓦匠在门外哼唱而来，他立刻将曲子记在了纸上。

1876年，列夫·托尔斯泰来到莫斯科，出席了一场由尼古拉·鲁宾斯坦主持的私人音乐会，其间演奏了这首「如歌的行板」。据在场的柴可夫斯基记述，托尔斯泰在聆听这一乐章时突然泪流满面，无法自制。音乐会结束后，托尔斯泰握着柴可夫斯基的手说：「我已经接触到了某种更贴近无限的东西。」这一场景是音乐史上最著名的「听众感动时刻」之一，也成为柴可夫斯基一生引以为傲的记忆。

「如歌的行板」后来被柴可夫斯基本人改编为弦乐队演奏版本，广泛用于音乐会，甚至获得了超越原版四重奏的知名度。这首乐章证明了柴可夫斯基在室内乐这一相对较小的体裁中同样具有超越时代的旋律天赋；它也是俄罗斯民间音乐如何通过一位作曲家的天才升华为普世性动人音乐的最佳范例。
famousScores.detailEn:
The First String Quartet was completed in 1871, and its second movement, Andante cantabile, is the best-known passage of the entire quartet and one of the most emotionally affecting movements in all string quartet literature. The movement's main theme derives from a Ukrainian folk song — Tchaikovsky is said to have heard a plasterer humming it outside a window at the Kamenka estate and immediately notated it.

In 1876, Leo Tolstoy came to Moscow and attended a private concert organized by Nicholas Rubinstein, at which the Andante cantabile was performed. According to Tchaikovsky's own account, Tolstoy was suddenly overcome with tears during the movement, unable to control himself. After the concert Tolstoy took Tchaikovsky's hand and said: 'I have been touched by something close to the infinite.' This scene is one of the most celebrated 'audience moved to tears' moments in music history, and a memory Tchaikovsky cherished for the rest of his life.

Tchaikovsky later arranged the Andante cantabile for string orchestra, and this version has become even more widely known than the original quartet. The movement proves that Tchaikovsky possessed transcendent melodic genius even in the relatively intimate medium of chamber music, and it stands as the finest example of how a Russian folk melody, through a composer's genius, can become universally moving music.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Tchaikovsky String Quartet No.1 Borodin Quartet

influence: 柴可夫斯基开创的俄罗斯交响传统由拉赫玛尼诺夫、普罗科菲耶夫和肖斯塔科维奇继承和发展。他的芭蕾音乐彻底改变了芭蕾艺术的地位。他的旋律天赋影响了好莱坞电影音乐的黄金时代——约翰·威廉斯等人的管弦乐写作与柴可夫斯基一脉相承。他是古典音乐中最「亲民」的伟大作曲家，入门听众的最佳引路人。
influenceEn: The Russian symphonic tradition Tchaikovsky established was carried forward by Rachmaninoff, Prokofiev, and Shostakovich. His ballet scores permanently transformed the status of the art form. His melodic genius influenced the golden age of Hollywood film scoring — the orchestral writing of John Williams and others is directly descended from Tchaikovsky. He remains the most accessible of the great composers in the classical canon, and the finest guide for newcomers to the art.
